title: Shared-score multivalue decode cluster for the Llama7B attention frontier
hypothesis: A shared-score multivalue cluster rooted at `attention_decode_score_multivalue_cluster_int8_m1x8_iterdiv` can preserve the existing signed-int8 Q/K/V, s32 score, LUT-softmax decode contract while eliminating the prior cluster's 16 repeated score fills by storing each M1x8 score row once per 8-token block and replaying it across all 16 value-slice groups.

direct_comparison.primary_question: How much of the local-cluster frontier loss comes from repeating score fill and store 16 times instead of filling once and replaying 16 eight-dimensional value slices from shared score state?
include: one M1x8 score-row fill per 8-token block
shared score storage written once and replayed across 16 value-slice groups
eight-dimensional streamed result beats for each value slice
one iterative divider shared across the replayed value path
unchanged signed-int8 Q/K/V inputs, signed-32 score accumulation, and LUT softmax semantics
repo-portable JSON and Markdown equivalence evidence before any physical sweep
exclude: do not erase or overwrite the committed local-cluster frontier evidence; revise it as a superseded architecture bound only
do not promote vectorless OpenROAD power as token energy
chip-level NoC, clock/reset, and HBM/DRAM composition remain outside this local-cluster step

required_evaluations.objective: Prove one score fill, one score-store write, 16 value-slice replays, streamed eight-dimension outputs, and one iterative divider preserve the integer Llama7B decode contract.

expected_result.reason: The new architecture changes score fill and value replay topology, so equivalence must pass before any PPA spending.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure Nangate45 timing, area, and vectorless power for the shared-score multivalue cluster once equivalence passes.

expected_result.reason: Vectorless power is needed for structural comparison, but token energy remains blocked until activity-backed evidence exists.

required_evaluations.objective: Re-run the shared-score multivalue cluster at 8 ns for the 2.5 mm die / 2.4 mm square core bridge evidence.

expected_result.reason: Collect the missing 8 ns Nangate45 evidence in 2.5 mm envelope while keeping current vectorless metrics as bounded frontier evidence.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure the exact-state activity calibration path with the unique binary-FSM 8 ns sweep, matched to the v2 physical architecture and configuration.

expected_result.reason: V2 remains the valid one-hot PPA baseline and is not invalidated. The base v3 flow completed, but remained one-hot because OpenROAD's initial hierarchical `synth -run :fine` omitted `SYNTH_ARGS`. R1 stabilized mode expansion so `decode_score_multivalue_cluster_v1_8ns_binary_fsm_v3_proxy_die_2500` is emitted as a single suffix and kept `SYNTH_ARGS=-nofsm`, yet still remained non-promotable for routed-state width mismatch. PR #1419 / merge 0d5317a1 propagates args to every synth call and validates exact `1_synth.v` state widths.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure the exact-state activity calibration path with the unique binary-FSM 8 ns sweep, matched to the v2 physical architecture and configuration.

expected_result.reason: R2 was superseded before dispatch: it retained r1's v3 sweep parameter hash, so `--skip_existing` could reuse the old one-hot output instead of exercising PR #1419. No r2 physical result is claimed.

required_evaluations.status_reason: R2 reused r1's sweep parameter hash, so `--skip_existing` could not distinguish the intended corrected binary-FSM mapping from the old one-hot output.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure the exact-state activity calibration path with the unique binary-FSM 8 ns sweep, matched to the v2 physical architecture and configuration.

expected_result.reason: R3 used the fresh v4 flow identity and reached physical implementation, but failed in global placement with RSZ-1008. It is non-promotable and supplies no successful PNR evidence; retained 3/4-bit synthesized-state widths are not claimed until issue #1416 supplies the diagnostic artifact.

required_evaluations.status_reason: Global placement failed with RSZ-1008; no successful PNR row or retained-state-width evidence is claimed.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure an 8 ns targeted-binary-FSM candidate that preserves normal Yosys FSM optimization while applying explicit binary encoding attributes only to the top and reducer state machines.

expected_result.reason: Use a fresh config and flow parameter hash with explicit binary FSM attributes and normal Yosys FSM optimization. Promotion still requires an exact successful 8 ns row and post-synthesis 3/4-bit state-width evidence; no retained widths are claimed before issue #1416 materializes that diagnostic.

required_evaluations.status_reason: Canceled after 31.8h runtime; targeted-binary is non-promotable as a physically feasible successor.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure an 8 ns explicit-onehot FSM candidate that keeps the v2 physical architecture and uses source-level one-hot state vectors for the cluster controller and multivalue reducer, with guarded exact post-synthesis 7/11-bit state widths and empty/default SYNTH_ARGS.

expected_result.reason: Use a fresh config and flow parameter hash with source-level explicit one-hot FSM state vectors, exact empty/default SYNTH_ARGS, exact-state diagnostic evidence bound to the selected metrics row and synthesized netlist, and no reliance on Yosys auto-recoding. Promotion still requires a successful 8 ns row with critical_path_ns<=8 and exact post-synthesis 7/11-bit state widths.

required_evaluations.status_reason: Non-physical pre-PPA failure: guard_config_selection_mismatch. The generator used config_explicit_onehot_fsm.json, but check_attention_decode_score_multivalue_cluster_guard.py hardcoded design-dir/config.json, rejected manifest fsm_encoding=explicit_onehot against the baseline config, and produced no physical evidence.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure an 8 ns explicit-onehot FSM candidate that keeps the v2 physical architecture and uses source-level one-hot state vectors for the cluster controller and multivalue reducer, with guarded exact post-synthesis 7/11-bit state widths and empty/default SYNTH_ARGS.

expected_result.reason: R1 retried the same explicit-onehot config/sweep/baseline after the guard config-selection fix and reached successful PNR, but the post-PNR strict explicit_onehot checker still failed. Because explicit_onehot_fsm_diagnostic.json was not transported from that run, the exact checker failure cause remains unavailable.

required_evaluations.status_reason: Successful PNR evidence exists for r1, but the strict post-synthesis checker failed and the diagnostic artifact was not transported, so the exact cause is not confirmed. The row is not promoted. A checker-only rescue remains conditional on preserving the evaluator's exact 1_synth.v and materializing the already DB-transported metrics artifact without rerunning OpenROAD; no dispatchable follow-up item is queued until that path exists.

remaining_abstractions_after_this_step: Score multiplier and shift derivation remain external inputs to the cluster boundary.
Vectorless power is a structural proxy only; token energy remains blocked until switching activity is measured.
The shared-score multivalue cluster still stops at one local block boundary and does not close chip-level transport or memory.
